attention4.ptxt: Brake fluid is extremely damaging to painted surfaces.
If brake fluid contacts a painted surface, immediately wipe it off and flush the surface thoroughly with water.

s1.ptxt: Hoist vehicle and remove rear wheels.
Remove rear vehicle height sensor bracket from rear axle.
Remove rear brake disc dust cover and caliper for each of right and left rear wheels.
Remove wheel speed sensor clamp (4), rear wheel speed sensor harness (5) and parking brake cable clamp (6) from each of right and left rear axle.
Remove rear wheel speed sensor and parking brake cable for each of right and left rear wheels.
Disconnect brake pipes (1) from brake hoses (2), and then remove E-ring (3) as shown.
Disconnect shock absorber lower end from rear axle and remove coil springs.
While supporting rear axle at both ends, remove rear axle bolts (1), and then remove rear axle from vehicle body by lowering floor jack gradually.

attention4.ptxt: The following bolt and nut are pre-coated with friction stabilizer. If these bolt and nut are reused, they may work loose.
Never reuse the following bolt and nut.

s1.ptxt: Place rear axle on floor jacks.
Then install rear axle with new rear axle bolts (1) and tighten bolts temporarily by hand.
Install coil spring and connect shock absorber lower end to rear axle.
Connect brake pipes to brake hoses, and then install E-rings. Tighten rear brake pipe flare nuts to specified torque.
Install wheel speed sensor clamp and parking brake cable clamp to rear axle.
Install rear wheel speed sensor and parking brake cable for each of right and left rear wheels.
Install rear brake disc dust cover and caliper for each of right and left rear wheels.
Install rear vehicle height sensor bracket to rear axle.
After installing brake parts, bleed air from brake system.
Adjust parking brake.
Install rear wheels.
Lower hoist and bounce vehicle up and down several times to stabilize suspension.
Tighten shock absorber lower nuts and rear axle bolts to specified torques.
Check that rear brake are free from dragging and proper braking is obtained.
Check all installed parts for brake fluid leakage.
